title: Why You Should Never Make Decisions After an Investor Meeting
description: If you feel like your brain is fried after talking to investors, you are not crazy. It is a real psychological phenomenon called "Mentor Whiplash" that confuses even experienced founders. I explain why this happens and how to fix it so you can sleep at night.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Raising SEIS? It’s Crunch Time Baby!
description: There are only 8 weeks left to close your round before the end of the financial year, so if you miss this window, you might have to wait months for the next one. Investors are looking to deploy cash right now to secure their tax relief, but you need to be ready with your Advance Assurance and allocation. Read the full post to understand exactly which investors are buying and fill out the form to get matched before the deadline.
author: Startup Pulse

title: The Comfortable Lie of "Pre-Seed" is Over
description: The "idea stage" is dead for 2026, so you need to stop waiting for funding and start building traction if you want to raise pre-seed capital. Investors are done with generic AI "wrappers," so the smart money is now flowing into specific, unsexy verticals with deep domain expertise. To survive, you must ruthlessly qualify investors to avoid "zombie VCs" who haven't written a check in months.
author: Startup Pulse

title: B2B Sales 101: Make Them Look Good and You’ll Get the Deal
description: Most founders think B2B sales are about logic and ROI, but they’re really about ego and emotion. You’re not selling to a company — you’re selling to a person who wants to look smart, get promoted, or feel in control. The faster you learn to sell to the human inside the machine, the faster your deals close.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Should You Be Raising Right Now?
description: The market’s tightening, and a correction isn’t a matter of if — it’s when. Founders now face a harsh reality: raise before the window closes or be forced to fight for scraps later. Those with real traction will thrive; those selling stories will finally get exposed.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Money Is Back on the Table
description: After months of slow movement, early-stage funding across the UK and Europe is finally picking up again, with several new funds launching back-to-back and investor optimism returning fast. It’s the best moment in a while for founders to raise — the money’s flowing, and those who act now can ride the wave before year-end.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Why Shiny Things Are a Founder’s Worst Enemy
description: Staying focused as a founder is one of the hardest — and most important — skills to build. This post shares what I’m still learning about prioritising what really matters, avoiding shiny distractions, and saying no (even when it’s uncomfortable).
author: Startup Pulse

title: You’re Not Supposed to Know Everything
description: Being a founder doesn’t mean knowing everything — it means learning fast and asking for help when you need it. This post is a reminder that you’re not behind, you’re just building something new (and that’s hard enough already).
author: Startup Pulse

title: Why Building a Business in the UK Feels Like a Punishment
description: Many founders in the UK feel like the system is designed to hold them back — from unfair VAT rules to the lack of any safety net when starting out. This post breaks down what’s broken, what other countries are doing better, and what practical steps founders can take right now.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Investors Are Not Your Friends
description: Most founders focus on pitch decks and intros, but the real danger lies in negotiation. This post breaks down how and when founders lose control of their company — especially around Series A — and how to push back with confidence. Based on Soma Pirityi’s masterclass, it’s a must-read for anyone fundraising.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Why It’s Taking Forever to Get to Series A
description: Startups are taking much longer to reach Series A and C, with US averages now at 2.5 years and European timelines also stretching, while graduation rates have dropped sharply. This shift is driven by macro headwinds, tighter investor scrutiny, and the rise of bridge rounds, forcing founders to operate leaner for longer. The winners will be those who adjust early, secure more runway, and hit investor-ready milestones before chasing the next big round.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Why Every AI Startup Needs to Become a Consulting Firm
description: AI startups can’t just sell models anymore — they need to deliver real outcomes. OpenAI’s $10M consulting arm shows that enterprise clients want full-stack solutions, not just clever tech. If you’re building in B2B, it’s time to roll up your sleeves and become the team that makes AI actually work.
author: Startup Pulse

title: If You Need Balance, Don’t Found a Startup
description: Most founders work 7 days a week—not because they’re chasing hustle culture, but because building something real takes relentless focus. Balance comes later (if ever); in the early stage, your startup is your life.
author: Startup Pulse

title: How to Engage with Investors
description: Most founders screw up investor conversations by treating them like ATMs, hiding problems, or failing to do basic homework. This is your blunt guide to avoiding those rookie mistakes — build real relationships, be brutally honest, and stop making it harder than it needs to be.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Sam Altman's Secret Playbook: What Every Founder Misses
description: Most founders chase shiny trends, but Sam Altman’s real playbook is all about nailing the boring, unsexy basics—like building a great product, hiring right, and obsessing over users. His advice isn’t new, just rarely followed—and that’s exactly why most startups fail.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Summer Fundraising Slowdown: Why It’s Real and How Founders Can Prepare
description: Summer is the worst time to raise — investors are offline, replies are slow, and deals stall. Smart founders use this downtime to prep: fix the deck, build traction, and line up a September sprint before the year-end freeze.
author: Startup Pulse

title: Getting in Bed with Bad Investors
description: + weekly list of handpicked startup events for founders and investors in London
author: Startup Pulse

title: Why Your Startup Isn’t Getting Investor Meetings
description: + weekly list of handpicked startup events for founders and investors in London
author: Startup Pulse

title: Approaching investors who back competitors
description: + weekly list of handpicked startup events for founders and investors in London
author: Startup Pulse
